questions.question: What is the standard duration for optimizing linear programming problems in the (8 hours) method?
questions.explanation: The (8 hours) method assumes that the optimization problem can be solved within 8 hours or less.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key assumption of the (8 hours) method?
options: All problems have an optimal solution
The number of variables is small
questions.explanation: The (8 hours) method assumes that the optimization problem is convex.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the (8 hours) method?
questions.explanation: The primary goal of the (8 hours) method is to reduce computational time while still finding an optimal or near-optimal solution.

questions.question: Which optimization technique is an alternative to the (8 hours) method?
questions.explanation: The cutting plane method is an alternative optimization technique that can be used when the (8 hours) method is not feasible.

questions.question: What is the typical application domain of the (8 hours) method?
questions.explanation: The (8 hours) method is typically applied to large-scale linear programming problems.
